title: Breea setosa
intro: Breea setosa, also known as Spilanthes oleracea, is a tropical herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to South America and widely distributed in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal and oral health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and anti-plaque properties, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation, mouth ulcers, and bad breath. The bioactive constituents of Breea setosa include spilanthol, a sesquiterpene compound with anti-inflammatory and anesthetic properties, as well as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Breea setosa are teas, tinctures, and oral health products, such as mouthwashes and toothpaste, which are used to promote oral health and prevent gum disease.
taxonomy: Breea setosa is classified within the genus Breea, which is part of the family Asteraceae. The species Breea setosa is native to South Africa. Breea setosa is often categorized within the tribe Cardueae, which includes various thistle and cardoon species. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species. Further research may be necessary to fully understand the classification and relationships within the Breea genus.
uses: Breea setosa has been found to have potential medicinal properties, with traditional uses including treatment for fever, headache, and gastrointestinal issues. In some regions, Breea setosa is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
